templates.name: Metroidvania (2D side-scroller)
templates.description: 2D metroidvania base: MetSys world map/minimap with a 2-room starter map, platformer player (coyote time, jump buffer, variable jump height), automatic room transitions, cell discovery, and the standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager groups, save_data() contract).

vendoredAddons.name: MetroidvaniaSystem
vendoredAddons.version: 1.5 (branch 4.6 — repo has no release tags; master tracks Godot 4.7-dev)

patches.replace:
if not ResourceUID.has_id(ResourceUID.text_to_id("uid://crxgu2q73va5y")):
		# Bootstrap import (godot --headless --import): the UID cache is not
		# built yet, so the editor UI cannot load. Skip it; the plugin loads
		# fully on the next editor start.
		return
	main = load("uid://crxgu2q73va5y").instantiate()
patches.reason: skip editor-UI creation during bootstrap --import runs where the UID cache does not exist yet

patches.replace:
if not is_instance_valid(main):
		return
	main.queue_free()
	remove_tool_menu_item("MetSys")
patches.reason: guard _exit_tree teardown for bootstrap/instant-quit editor runs where the UI was never created

primitives.note: the platformer action set is already baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only to add gamepad rebinding UI
the player carries a Camera2D whose limits are clamped to the current MetSys room; swap in the sidescroller rig for deadzone/look-ahead
player controller already implements coyote time + jump buffering; use game-feel for shake/hitstop when combat is added

Tileset for rooms (16px or 32px grid, tileable: ground, walls, platforms)
Parallax background layers (2-3, per biome)
Door / passage frame sprite (marks MetSys border type 1)
Map theme override art only if the bundled MetSys 'Exquisite' minimap theme clashes with the palette

templates.name: Point-and-Click Adventure
templates.description: Classic point-and-click adventure base on Popochiu: one starter room with a clickable prop and a walkable area, a player character wired as PC, the simple_click GUI (look/interact verbs, inventory bar, dialog lines), transition layer, and Popochiu's room/character/inventory/dialog pipeline ready in the editor dock.

primitives.note: generate a theme.tres from the game palette and apply it to game/gui/gui.tscn to de-generic the Popochiu GUI
room/prop descriptions and dialog trees; Popochiu dialogs live in game/dialogs/

Player character walk/idle/talk spritesheets (4-direction or side view, ~32x64 px)
Prop sprites with readable silhouettes (transparent PNG, placed on the background)
Cursor sprite set (active/look/wait variants)
Inventory item icons (16x16 or 32x32 px)

templates.name: FPS / Immersive Sim (3D)
templates.description: First-person immersive-sim base on COGITO: full player controller (sprint/crouch/slide/stairs/ladders, attributes: health/stamina/visibility-stealth, grid inventory, quick slots, wieldables), component-based interaction system, quest system, EasyMenus main/pause menus with save slots — plus one blockout test room with the COGITO player, an openable door and a carryable health-potion pickup wired as live interactables. Engine pin note (tested 2026-07-11): COGITO v1.1.6 targets Godot 4.5.1 and imports clean there; on 4.6.1 its scene manager fails to parse (GDScript analyzer cannot resolve CogitoQuestManager.active/completed/failed typed members, breaking SaveSlotButton.gd) — so this template pins 4.5 and must be scaffolded/validated with a 4.5.x Godot. Standard NoxDev ABI on top (Master/Music/SFX buses by name, player/game_manager groups, save_data() contract on GameManager, pause action alongside COGITO's menu action).

vendoredAddons.version: 1.1.6 (Codeberg is the live upstream — GitHub mirror's tags stop at v1.1.5; targets Godot 4.5.1-stable)

templates.name: Visual Novel (with dice checks)
templates.description: Visual-novel base on Dialogue Manager 3 plus a pen-and-paper dice layer: title scene, story scene with blockout stage, a VN textbox (name plate, typewriter DialogueLabel, response menu, click/accept to advance), one .dialogue file with a branch and a skill-check gate, and a SkillCheck autoload (d20 + stat modifier vs DC, crit rules, seedable RNG) whose dice-roll popup is awaited as a Dialogue Manager mutation (`do SkillCheck.skill_check("mind", 12)` then `if SkillCheck.last_success`). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

primitives.note: story content lives in dialogue/*.dialogue — Dialogue Manager syntax (titles, responses, conditions, mutations); the skill_check gate in intro.dialogue is the pattern to copy for new checks

VN portrait pipelines (talking portraits, expression sets) for the Stage placeholders in story.tscn

templates.name: Deckbuilder (Slay-the-Spire-like)
templates.description: Roguelike deckbuilder (Slay-the-Spire-like) on chun92/card-framework. The COMBAT is JSON-defined cards (Strike/Cleave/Defend/Insight/Surge, generated blockout art) + a full draw/play/discard loop (5-card hand refill, 3 energy/turn, drag-to-play resolving damage/block/draw/energy, discard reshuffles when dry, End Turn discards the hand and the enemy attacks through your block). Wrapping it is a full ROGUELIKE RUN engine in GameManager (pure, seedable, headless-testable): the game opens on a MAP hub (scenes/map.tscn) — a generated, connected floor-by-floor node DAG (combat / elite / event / rest / boss) you route through; your HP, gold, deck and relics PERSIST across fights; wins earn gold + a card reward (pick 1 of 3), elites/boss grant relics (bonus energy / start-block / heal-on-kill, queried by combat), rest nodes heal, the boss ends the run. Combat reads the run when one is active (persisted HP, grown deck, the node's scaled encounter, relic mods) and reports the result back; with no active run the combat scene still plays standalone against the default Cog-Golem, so the base loop + its boot probe are unchanged. Kit decision (2026-07-11): DesirePathGames/Slay-The-Robot was evaluated per the roadmap but it is a complete game (no addon payload) — kept as an MIT reference; card-framework is the vendorable engine (containers, drag-drop, JSON factory; Godot 4.6 pin).

vendoredAddons.name: Card Framework
vendoredAddons.version: 1.4.0 (upstream targets Godot 4.6+; script-only kit, no editor plugin)

patches.replace: pass # NoxDev: upstream printed every preloaded card here — one line per card per boot
patches.reason: silence per-card preload prints that flood the log on every boot

replace the generated blockout card art in cards/art/ (150x210 per card + card_back) — filenames are referenced from cards/data/*.json
juice the resolve moments: shake/flash on enemy hit, punch-scale on the banner — hook play_area.card_played

assetPlanHints: Card face art (150x210 px per card: frame + illustration window + name/description bands)
Card back (150x210 px, deck identity pattern)
Enemy portraits (~256x256 px, one per encounter)
Table background (1280x720, low-contrast felt/board so cards stay readable)
UI icons (energy bolt, block shield, HP heart, draw/discard pile markers)
Card SFX (draw swish, play thunk, shuffle riffle, enemy hit)

templates.name: Top-Down Action (Hotline-Miami-like)
templates.description: Top-down action base, pure first-party (no vendored kit — build-cheap per the roadmap survey): CharacterBody2D player with 8-directional movement, mouse aim, hitscan raycast shot with tracer, dash with cooldown and health/respawn; three shootable practice targets; a NavigationAgent2D chaser enemy pathing across the arena's NavigationRegion2D with contact damage; arena with walls, HUD (HP + targets left), and the standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, topdown input action set).

wire shake/hitstop into player.shot_fired and take_hit — the signals are already emitted
the arena is a single hand-built room; use world-layout to generate level floors, then re-bake each level's NavigationRegion2D

templates.name: Isometric ARPG (Diablo-like)
templates.description: Isometric action-RPG base, pure first-party (per the Wave-2 survey the genre's core loop is build-cheap): click-to-move player (NavigationAgent2D, hold-to-repath Diablo style) on an isometric TileMapLayer (diamond-down 128x64 tileset, floor painted in code from a 2-tile atlas), an ability bar with a melee swing and a cooldown AoE nova, three chasing enemies (the top-down-action chaser archetype), and a loot system: Pandora-style item database in data/items.json, weighted rarity roll (common/magic/rare/legendary) scaling the item's base stat, rarity-tinted ground pickups collected on walk-over into a run inventory. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

primitives.note: hitstop on melee connects and shake on nova — hook player.ability_used and enemy take_hit flashes
the diamond arena is one hand-authored NavigationPolygon; use world-layout for dungeon floors, then re-author each floor's nav polygon around its walls
town NPCs / vendors import as Pandora entities — the same JSON style items.json already uses

templates.name: Arcade Racing (checkpoint laps)
templates.description: Arcade racing base on Godot-Easy-Vehicle-Physics (raycast vehicle: engine/torque curve, gearbox, per-wheel suspension and slip, arcade handling preset) plus first-party race logic: the addon's arcade car + VehicleController wired to the NoxDev input actions (throttle/brake/steer/handbrake/shift), a chase camera, a flat blockout track with three ordered checkpoint gates and a start/finish line, lap counting (timer armed on first line crossing, lap completed once all gates passed in order), lap/best-lap timing recorded into GameManager flags, and a HUD (lap, time, gate, speed/gear). Kit note (pinned 2026-07-11): upstream has no release tags — pinned to main @ c392257f (Aug 2025, latest); script-only addon, no editor plugin. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

templates.name: Gamebook (solo pen-and-paper RPG)
templates.description: Fighting-Fantasy-style gamebook base on Dialogue Manager 3 (same pin as visual-novel): keyed numbered passages in dialogue/book.dialogue (titles are passage numbers, choices read 'turn to N'), an adventure-sheet autoload (SKILL 1d6+6 / STAMINA 2d6+12 / LUCK 1d6+6 rolled per run, provisions, inventory LIST with add/has/remove used straight from dialogue mutations and conditions), and a 2d6 roll-UNDER dice layer (Dice.test('skill'), Dice.test_luck() with LUCK attrition, snake-eyes/double-six crit rules, seedable RNG) whose dice popup is awaited as a Dialogue Manager mutation. Ships a 6-passage starter adventure (The Toll Bridge) demonstrating passage jumps, an item-gated choice, a SKILL test with STAMINA damage on failure, and a LUCK test. Title screen rolls a fresh sheet; the story scene shows a live sheet panel. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

templates.name: Farming Sim (Stardew-like, 2D)
templates.description: 2D farming base on the TimeTick GDExtension (shoyguer/time-tick — vendored from the sha256-pinned 1.1 release zip; prebuilt binaries for Windows/Linux/macOS/Android/iOS/Web, targets Godot 4.6): a TimeSystem autoload wrapping TimeTick (tick -> minute -> hour -> day at 0.1s/game-minute, derived 28-day seasons, sleep_to_next_day(), save/load of the clock), a first-party day/night CanvasModulate tint sampling a 24-hour gradient off the clock (day_started/night_started at 06:00/19:00), a 20x12 farm TileMapLayer (grass/tilled/watered atlas painted in code) with till/plant/harvest, one data-driven Crop resource (turnip.tres: 4 growth stages, 1 day/stage, blockout stage visuals) whose growth recomputes from day deltas on TimeSystem.day_changed (idempotent), a farmer (top-down movement + one contextual E action: harvest > plant > till on the tile underfoot), and a clock/season/harvest HUD. Kit note (2026-07-11): the survey's day/night pick (maetzemax day-and-night-cycle 0.3.0) was evaluated and is 3D-only (CycleController extends Node3D driving DirectionalLight3D + WorldEnvironment sky) — it does not fit a 2D TileMap farm, so the 2D tint layer is first-party; use that addon instead if the project goes 3D. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, pause action).

vendoredAddons.version: 1.1 (GDExtension release zip — repo tag 1.1 @ 796506ad is the C++ source; binaries only ship in the release asset, hence the archive pin)

templates.name: Horror FPS (first-person horror)
templates.description: First-person horror base: the COGITO immersive-sim stack (same vendor pins as fps-immersive — full player controller, component interactions, inventory, quests, menus) plus resonate adaptive music (GitLab continuation of the archived hugemenace repo — MusicBank with a 2-stem 'ambient' track: calm pad always on, dread drone gated) and a first-party Sanity autoload (0-100 stat, ambient drain, safe-zone restore via Area3D light pools, scare() hits, hysteresis low-sanity thresholds). The room script wires sanity to a vignette overlay shader (intensity = 1 - sanity) and to the dread stem (enable_stem under the low threshold, disable on recovery) — stat, presentation, and audio stay decoupled. Test room: night-lit blockout with the COGITO player, openable door, carryable pickup, one safe lamp pool, committed placeholder stem WAVs. Engine pin note: inherits fps-immersive's 4.5 pin (COGITO v1.1.6 targets Godot 4.5.1; its quest manager breaks the 4.6 GDScript analyzer) — scaffold and validate with a 4.5.x Godot. Standard NoxDev ABI on top.

templates.name: Voxel Sandbox (Minecraft-like, 3D)
templates.description: Blocky voxel sandbox on Zylann/godot_voxel, GDExtension edition (vendored from the sha256-pinned v1.6x release zip — 'Voxel Tools 1.6 GDExtension for Godot 4.5+', prebuilt Windows/Linux/macOS/iOS/Android binaries, self-registering, works on the official engine build; validated on official 4.6.1). The terrain is built entirely in code (scripts/main.gd) so the scene stays parseable without the extension and the setup is diffable: a 2-model VoxelBlockyLibrary (air + colored cube) on VoxelMesherBlocky over a flat TYPE-channel VoxelGeneratorFlat, streamed around a VoxelViewer attached to the player. First-person fly controller (mouse capture/look, WASD + Space/Shift) with dig (LMB) and place (RMB) through VoxelToolTerrain raycasts; edits counted into GameManager flags + HUD. GDExtension notes: no FastNoise2 in the extension edition; exports work out of the box on official templates. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contract, pause action).

vendoredAddons.version: 1.6 GDExtension (release tag v1.6x @ 595f52ee; the plain v1.6 release is the module edition — a full custom engine build — do not confuse them)

templates.name: Vampire Survivors-like (auto-attack swarm roguelite)
templates.description: Auto-attack swarm roguelite base, pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no clearly-maintained MIT swarm kit exists — BulletUpHell covers dense projectile patterns, not enemy swarms, and the genre's loop is build-cheap): WASD survivor whose weapon fires on a timer at the nearest enemy (pooled first-party projectiles), an enemy-swarm director spawning timed off-screen ring waves that scale in count/speed/health — all active enemies moved by ONE loop with pooling and no physics bodies, comfortably 200+ — with contact damage on a per-enemy cooldown, XP gems on kill with magnet pickup (same pooled single-loop pattern), and a level-up flow: data-driven Upgrade resources (res://resources/upgrades/*.tres — damage/fire rate/move speed/magnet radius/max HP) offered 3 at a time in a pausing choice UI (click or keys 1-3, level-ups queue). Survival timer + kill counter HUD; death opens a run summary (time/kills/level, best-kills + best-time recorded to GameManager flags, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

primitives.note: movement actions are baked into the skeleton's project.godot (the weapon aims itself — no aim/attack actions needed); re-run only for rebinding UI
hook player.weapon_fired / take_hit flashes and spawner.enemy_killed for shake, hitstop, and kill pops — the signals are already emitted
enemy death bursts and gem sparkles at spawner.enemy_killed / gems.gem_collected; keep them pooled like everything else in the swarm

Enemy swarm spritesheets (2-3 silhouette families, ~24-32 px walk cycles; palette-distinct from player)
XP gem sprite (8-12 px, readable at speed; tier tints for bigger values)
Projectile sprites (8-16 px, high-contrast) + muzzle/impact flashes (small, additive)
Ground tile texture (tileable 64px, low-contrast so the swarm stays readable)
Upgrade icons (48x48 px, one per resources/upgrades entry) + level-up/death UI panel art
SFX: shot, enemy death squish, gem pickup chime, level-up sting, player hurt

templates.name: Zelda-like (top-down action-adventure)
templates.description: Top-down action-adventure base (classic Zelda structure), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no clearly-maintained MIT Zelda kit exists, and the adventure layer is build-cheap on top of the top-down-action archetype): a 4-directional hero with a sword-arc sweep (distance+angle hit-test against the enemies group), an item button driving the equipped-item slot (ships the boomerang — out-and-return flight that stuns enemies), and a 3-room world on a screen grid with Zelda-style room transitions: the camera snaps to whichever room the player walks into and each room wakes only its own enemies; rooms build their floor/perimeter walls (with doorway gaps) in code so scenes stay lean. Dungeon fundamentals: a small-key pickup, a locked door that consumes the key on bump, a latching pressure-plate puzzle opening a switch door, and a treasure chest granting + equipping the boomerang via the interact button — every key/door/plate/chest writes a GameManager flag so world state survives room transitions and scene reloads. Two enemy archetypes on a shared chassis (ping-pong patroller, straight-line chaser) with touch damage on cooldown, sword take_hit, boomerang stun, and chance heart drops; hearts row + key count + equipped-item HUD. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts — player saves hearts/keys/items, GameManager saves flags — scalable_text, pause action).

primitives.note: the adventure action set (move, attack=Space, item=Shift, interact=E) is baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only for rebinding UI
hook player.attacked / take_hit flashes and door.opened for hitstop, shake and door-rumble — the signals are already emitted

rooms are screen-grid instances of room.tscn (position = grid coord * 1152x648); use world-layout to plan dungeon floors, then place room instances with matching doorway gaps and a door on each shared boundary

assetPlanHints: Hero spritesheet (~32x32 px, 4-direction walk + sword swing + item throw)
Enemy spritesheets (patroller + chaser families, ~32x32 px walk cycles; palette-distinct from hero)
Dungeon/overworld tileset (32px grid: floor variants, walls with top face; replaces the code-built Polygon2D rooms)
Door sprites (locked with keyhole, switch-barred, open frame; 64x128 to match the doorway gap)
Item + pickup sprites (small key, heart, boomerang) and 20x20 HUD item icons
Chest sprite (closed/open, ~48x36) and pressure-plate tile
SFX: sword swipe, boomerang whoosh loop, door unlock, chest fanfare, heart/key pickup, hero hurt

templates.name: Crowd Rush (hypercasual crowd-runner)
templates.description: Hypercasual 3D crowd-runner base (Count-Masters-like), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no maintained MIT crowd-runner kit exists and the genre is build-cheap: one MultiMesh, disc-formation math, count arithmetic): an auto-forward crowd of capsule units running a straight track with horizontal steering (A/D-arrows or mouse-x), every unit drawn by ONE MultiMesh in a phyllotaxis disc around the leader — no per-unit nodes, no physics bodies, 400 slots allocated so 200+ units stay cheap. Multiplier/add gates in choice pairs ("+10"/"-5", "-5"/"x2") consumed by the leader's lane on crossing; a spike-strip obstacle that kills the individual units sweeping through its box; a blocking enemy crowd resolved by 1:1 annihilation at a fixed rate (the bigger crowd survives with exactly the difference, a tie wipes the run); and a finish line + end-tower comparison (strictly more survivors than the tower count = win, score = surviving units). Crowd-count/distance HUD plus floating 3D count labels over both crowds; finish or wipe opens a run summary (survivors/tower/distance, best-survivors + best-distance flags on GameManager, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

primitives.note: the steer_left/steer_right + pause action set is baked into the skeleton's project.godot and mouse-x steering is built into crowd.gd; re-run only for rebinding UI
hook crowd.gate_applied / units_lost and enemy clash_started for gate pops, clash shake and kill ticks — the signals are already emitted
unit poofs on units_lost, gate bursts on gate_applied, confetti on finish_line.finished; keep them pooled — the crowd itself never allocates in the hot loop
the track is a straight strip of gate pairs / obstacles / enemy crowds ordered by z; layout work = placing (or generating) more of those nodes along a longer Track mesh

assetPlanHints: Runner character model (low-poly ~1-2k tris, run cycle; ONE mesh swapped into the crowd MultiMesh — every unit shares it)
Enemy runner variant (palette/silhouette swap of the runner, same single-mesh constraint)
Gate arch + panel art (translucent good/bad panels with big readable operation text)
Obstacle set (spike strip, saw blade, gap decals; keep kill AABBs matching the visuals)
Track + rail textures (tileable, low-contrast so the crowd reads) and a skybox gradient
Finish gate + end-tower model (defender count readable from afar on the front plate)
SFX: unit-gain pop, unit-loss squish, good/bad gate chimes, clash brawl loop, finish fanfare

templates.name: FPS Classic (arena shooter)
templates.description: Doom/Quake-style arena shooter base, pure first-party (no vendored kit — deliberately NOT the COGITO stack: fps-immersive/horror-fps are the slow immersive-sim lane on a 4.5 pin, this is the fast run-and-gun lane on 4.6): a Quake-ish first-person controller (WASD + mouselook, sprint, ground friction + Quake accelerate, small air-speed cap for real air control/strafe-jumps, held-jump auto-hop that skips landing friction so bunny-hops keep speed), two arena weapons on ammo pools with switch (Q/1/2) — a pellet-spread hitscan shotgun (seedable RNG) and a projectile rocket launcher whose ray-swept rocket deals distance-falloff splash plus player knockback (self-splash included = rocket jumps) — health/armor with Quake armor absorption, a code-built 40x40m arena (pillars, ramp, raised rocket-perch platform), four walk-over item spawners (health/armor/shells/rockets) on respawn countdowns that refuse pickups the player can't use, two enemy archetypes on a shared chassis with raycast-feeler chase steering (no nav bake, robust headless): a melee rusher and a ranged shooter firing plasma bolts with LOS checks. A wave director spawns escalating waves at ring points (wave n = n+1 rushers + n shooters), tracks kills, and death opens a run summary (kills/wave, best_kills + best_wave flags on GameManager, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

primitives.note: the arena action set (move, jump, sprint, fire, weapon_next/1/2, pause) is baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only for rebinding UI
hook weapons.fired for camera kick, player.took_damage for hurt flash/shake, and waves.enemy_killed for kill pops — the signals are already emitted
the arena is one code-built room (arena.gd _add_box calls); use world-layout for real arena graybox, keep everything on layer 1 'world' so hitscan/feeler/LOS rays keep working
shotgun blast / rocket whoosh+boom / pickup blips on the ABI SFX bus — fire from weapons.fired, projectile.exploded, item picked_up/respawned

Enemy models (rusher + shooter silhouettes, ~3k tris; one mesh swap per archetype in enemy_base._build_body)
Projectile + impact VFX (rocket trail, explosion flipbook, plasma bolt glow, shotgun muzzle flash)
Pickup models (medkit, armor shard, shell box, rocket crate; keep the spinner + color coding readable)
HUD art (crosshair variants, health/armor/ammo icons) and a stats-bar reskin
SFX: shotgun blast, rocket launch/explosion, plasma zap, pickup blips, hurt grunts, enemy death gibs

templates.name: Dungeon Crawler Classic (grid-based first-person)
templates.description: Grid-based first-person dungeon crawler base (Lands of Lore: The Throne of Chaos / Eye of the Beholder structure), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no maintained MIT grid-crawler kit exists and the genre is build-cheap: the whole illusion is grid math + tweens): the camera IS the party — a Node3D stepped cell-by-cell (forward/back/strafe, 90-degree turns, each smoothed with a short tween) through a dungeon built entirely in code from one ASCII map constant (14x13 starter: rooms, corridors, a plain bump-open door, a locked door + key, a lever-opened secret wall, torch-lit fog). NO physics anywhere — movement, door bumps, lever pulls, pickups, combat reach and enemy pathing are all grid queries, which keeps everything headless-robust and deterministic (zero RNG). Party of 3 (two front-row melee + a back-row caster whose 3-cell spark bolt costs MP): per-member HP/MP, per-member attack buttons with cooldowns (portrait click or hotkeys 1-3), front row soaks enemy melee, back row exposed only when both fronts fall. Real-time enemies occupy cells, step toward the party on move timers (greedy axis chase respecting doors/occupancy) and melee when cardinally adjacent. Pickups on walk-over (key; potions drunk via button heal the lowest-HP member); doors/lever/pickups write GameManager flags and self-restore so the dungeon stays solved across reloads. Classic bottom-bar HUD: scrolling message log, 3 portrait panels (ColorRect placeholder + HP/MP bars — Studio-bound art slots for the lol-vga style pack), compass from facing, keys/potions/kills. Party wipe opens a run summary (kills, secret found, best-kills flag, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

primitives.note: the crawler action set (WASD step/strafe, Q/E or arrows turn, 1-3 member attacks, R potion, Space interact, pause) is baked into the skeleton's project.godot; re-run only for rebinding UI
the bottom bar is the genre's face — theme the log panel, portrait frames and HP/MP bars from the game palette (the lol-ui-frame-v2 bench ref is the target anatomy)
the dungeon is one ASCII constant in dungeon.gd — layout work means editing/generating MAP strings (rooms, corridors, D/d/S/L/K/P/E markers), not placing meshes
hook party.took-a-hit via member_changed / take_enemy_hit messages for screen shake, and dungeon.door_opened / secret_opened for rumble — step/turn tweens are exports to tune

assetPlanHints: Wall/floor/ceiling texture set (VGA-256 Westwood look, 2m repeat — bench/lol-corridor-v2 is the style ref; nxdv_vga256 LoRA lane for scene-level art)
3 party portraits (~128x128, painted VGA style — bench/lol-portrait-v2 ref; replaces the ColorRect placeholder slots in the HUD)
Bottom-bar UI frame (parchment/stone chrome with portrait windows, log panel and compass — bench/lol-ui-frame-v2 ref)
Enemy billboard sprites or low-poly models (2-3 silhouettes; replaces the capsule in enemy.gd _build_body)
Door/lever/pickup props (wooden door leaf, locked variant, wall lever, key, potion flask; GLB or billboards)
SFX: footstep thud per step, door creak/unlock, lever clunk, stone grinding (secret), sword hit, spark cast, potion gulp, party-member hurt/fall

templates.name: FF Gamebook (illustrated Fighting-Fantasy book)
templates.description: Illustrated Fighting-Fantasy gamebook — the gamebook template's presentation evolution (Veritas Tales / classic FF book plates), same Dialogue Manager 3 pin, adventure sheet and 2d6 roll-under dice layer underneath: every passage is an ILLUSTRATED PAGE — large illustration plate on top, passage text + 'turn to N' choice buttons below, adventure-sheet bar, dice tray rolling over the page for tests. NEW core system: the asset-binding manifest (assets.manifest.json in the project root) — per-slot entries {slotId, kind: illustration|ui|audio, policy: static|generated|rule, workflowId (zit-txt2img), stylePack (veritas-ink), promptTemplate ({passage_summary}-templated for rule slots), source (dialogue+passage+summary), file, provenance {seed, params, generatedAt}} covering one slot per passage plate plus UI chrome (ui/page_frame, ui/dice_tray) and audio/page_turn; the AssetBinder autoload reads it and the page binds each passage's slot on entry — deterministic ColorRect placeholders until the Studio asset board generates real plates and writes file+provenance back (zero code changes to adopt them). Single-player core, multiplayer-READY architecture: ALL passage state, choices and dice results route through the SessionState autoload — advance_passage() (first mutation of every passage), choose() (the response buttons' route), roll()/roll_luck() (awaits the dice layer, records to roll_log) — documented as the future ENet sync point, with DM-seat hooks (dm_push_passage, dm_override_roll) shipped as documented no-ops. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups — GameManager, Sheet, Dice and SessionState all persist — save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

multiplayer.transportNote: ENet for LAN (default, validated). Switch transport to 'websocket' for one desktop+web code path (spec Phase 3, web-shippable); WebRTC for web realtime is spec Phase 5 (not bundled).
multiplayer.arbitrationNote: 'leader' resolves party choices immediately and works with the UNMODIFIED book scene (validated). 'vote' and 'dm-confirm' are fully implemented but defer the commit until a majority/DM approves — their deferred-commit UX needs a small MP-aware book hook (connect NetBridge.choice_committed/remote_advance and skip the optimistic walk), a Phase-2 finish. DM push/override work in every mode.
multiplayer.note: Opt-in: NOT applied by default scaffolding. Advertises 'Playable together' to the Studio genre picker; run the netcode primitive to inject it. Offline play is untouched (the SessionState guard is inert when Net.active is false).

validated.note: Injected into a fresh 4.6.1 scaffold: editor import clean (zero parse errors), nox_netcode API self-probe OK (host/seats/DM-authority/seed/teardown), single-player boot probe byte-identical (guard inert offline). True two-peer sync needs two running instances — see addons/nox_netcode/README.md manual steps; not validatable headless.

vendoredAddons.name: Dialogue Manager
vendoredAddons.version: 3.10.4 (DM 3.x line, requires Godot 4.4+ — same pin as the gamebook and visual-novel templates)

primitives.note: the book lives in dialogue/book.dialogue — every passage starts with `do SessionState.advance_passage("passage_N")` (that line is what binds the plate); when you add a passage, add its illustration/passage_N slot to assets.manifest.json with a source.summary for the prompt rule

do NOT hand-wire art paths into scenes — fill manifest slots instead: generate with each slot's workflowId + stylePack (veritas-ink) + promptTemplate, write the asset into the project and set the slot's file + provenance; AssetBinder picks it up on next boot
generate a theme.tres from the game palette for buttons/labels/sheet bar; the page frame and dice tray ART come from their manifest ui/* slots, so theme the widgets, not the chrome

OPT-IN multiplayer (build-order #12). Run `skills/netcode/tools/netcode_gen.py inject --project <dir> --profile authority-turn --transport enet` to make the book playable together: copies the nox_netcode addon into addons/, registers the Net + NetBridge autoloads, writes the [nox_netcode] settings block, and applies the pinned SessionState guard patch — advance_passage/choose/roll route through the host (host validates + broadcasts, clients render off signals) and the two DM-seat no-op hooks become real host-side dm_push_passage/dm_override_roll (require_dm). Idempotent. No netfox vendoring needed (native @rpc suffices for authority-turn). Single-player stays byte-identical because the guard is inert when Net.active is false.

Page frame / parchment page background (ui/page_frame slot: aged parchment, ornate ink filigree border, empty center)
Dice tray panel art (ui/dice_tray slot: worn leather tray seen from above, empty center)

Title screen key art (1280x720, book-cover composition)

templates.name: Top-Down Action (Unity)
templates.description: Unity port of the top-down-action archetype (Hotline-Miami-like), pure first-party: Rigidbody2D player with 8-directional movement, mouse aim, hitscan Physics2D raycast shot with LineRenderer tracer, dash with cooldown and health/respawn; three shootable practice targets; a direct-steering chaser enemy with contact damage (Unity has no first-party 2D navmesh — see TEMPLATE.md); code-built demo scene via the NoxBootstrap editor script (no committed .unity files); GameManager singleton with world flags and JSON save/load mirroring the NoxDev ABI save_data() contract via ISaveable.

templates.name: City Builder (grid economy, 2D)
templates.description: Grid-based city-builder base (pure first-party Godot, no addons): a GameManager autoload owns the economy - gold/food/population + a data-driven building catalogue (House +2 pop/-1 food, Farm +3 food, Market +4 gold, needs population) - with place()/demolish()/tick() as headless-testable logic; a 16x9 build grid drawn in code (blockout colours) with left-click place / right-click demolish / Esc pause, a resource HUD, and an affordability-gated building palette. NoxDev ABI: Master/Music/SFX buses, GameManager in game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (the whole city serialises), pause input, scalable_text HUD. Economy loop: farms feed houses, houses make population, population powers markets, markets make gold.

templates.name: Tower Defense (lane + towers, 2D)
templates.description: Classic tower-defense base (pure first-party Godot, no addons): a GameManager autoload owns gold/lives/wave + a data-driven tower catalogue (Arrow: cheap/fast/weak; Cannon: pricey/slow/strong) + place/demolish/award-kill/lose-life/begin-wave + scaling curves (enemy_count/hp per wave) as headless-testable logic; a playfield (td.gd) with a waypoint lane enemies walk, buildable cells clear of the corridor, real-time enemy movement + tower targeting/firing (nearest-in-range, per-tower cooldown, instant-hit + fire flash), a wave spawner (Start-wave button, re-enables when the field clears), enemy HP bars, resource HUD, and a tower palette. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (economy + towers persist), pause input, scalable_text. Loop: kills pay gold, leaks cost lives, waves scale.

templates.name: TCG Duel (turn-based card game, 2D)
templates.description: Collectible-card duel base (pure first-party Godot, no addons): the whole rules engine in a GameManager autoload as pure, seedable, headless-testable logic - two players with life/mana curves, shuffled decks, hands, and boards; a data-driven card catalogue (Recruit/Archer/Knight/Golem creatures + a Bolt spell) shuffled by a seeded RNG; turn flow (mana +1 to 10, refill, draw, ready board), can_play/play_card (summoning sickness; spell hits hero or a creature), attack (hero or a trade, dead cleared), end_turn, win detection, fatigue, and a greedy AI opponent. duel.gd renders both boards + your hand + life/mana + end-turn/new-duel, click a card to play, click a ready creature then a target to attack. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole board persists), pause input, scalable_text.

templates.name: Obby Multiplayer (2D obstacle course, netcode drop-in)
templates.description: A 2D obstacle-course platformer (obby) that ships the nox_netcode realtime drop-in pre-wired. The COURSE is built entirely in code from data (platforms, ordered checkpoints, hazards, a finish, a kill line); GameManager holds the run as pure, headless-testable logic (monotonic checkpoint progress with anti-skip, deaths, a live/best time, save/load). It runs two ways from one codebase: OFFLINE it spawns one local CharacterBody2D avatar and handles checkpoint/respawn/finish itself (a complete single-player obby, no multiplayer dependency); ONLINE (a nox_netcode session running) the NetSpawner spawns one avatar per peer via MultiplayerSpawner+MultiplayerSynchronizer and checkpoint/respawn/finish route through NetEvents so the host arbitrates an authoritative, cheat-resistant race. Vendored addon: nox_netcode (Net autoload, realtime profile, ENet transport). NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (best time persists), pause input, scalable_text.

bundledAddons.name: nox_netcode
bundledAddons.note: The NoxDev realtime multiplayer drop-in (Net autoload + MultiplayerSpawner/Synchronizer avatars + host-validated NetEvents), shipped IN the skeleton (not cloned at scaffold) because the obby is its reference realtime consumer. Keep it in sync with the skill's copy. True 2-peer sync needs two running instances (see TEMPLATE.md).

templates.name: Noir Detective (investigation + deduction, 2D)
templates.description: A film-noir detective game where the play IS deduction (Lacuna / Snatcher lineage), not twitch. The whole CASE is a data-driven catalogue in a GameManager autoload as pure, headless-testable logic: suspects, locations, clues (staged per location), and deductions (each unlocked by the right clue set). You investigate locations to surface clues, combine the right clues into deductions (motive / means / opportunity), and only once the deduction chain is complete may you accuse — noir plays fair, no naming a name on a hunch; accuse the real culprit and the case closes solved, accuse wrong and they walk. The investigation scene (noir.gd) builds a three-column casebook in code in a noir palette (Investigate / Casebook / Deductions + Accuse) and only reads the engine. Ships a complete sample case, 'The Neon Alibi'. Swap the SUSPECTS/LOCATIONS/CLUES/DEDUCTIONS/CULPRIT consts to author a new case with zero UI changes. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (an in-progress case — clues found, deductions made — persists), pause + restart input, scalable_text.

primitives.note: give each suspect a persona/voice via companion-npcs + Dialogue Manager for interrogation scenes between deductions
swap the code UI for real location art + suspect portraits (recipes: noir scene + noir portrait presets via zit-txt2img)

Clue / evidence item icons via qwen-icon

Rain / jazz / gunshot ambience + SFX

templates.name: Match Three (grid puzzle / minigame, 2D)
templates.description: A match-3 grid puzzle (Bejeweled/Candy-Crush lineage) — the classic dating-sim/gacha minigame — with the whole BOARD engine in a GameManager autoload as pure, seedable, headless-testable logic: an 8x8 grid of gem types generated with NO pre-made matches and a guaranteed legal move; try_swap validates an adjacent swap (only a swap that makes a line of 3+ commits, else it reverts), then resolves the full cascade — clear matched runs (score = gems x 10 x the chain step, so deeper cascades pay more), gravity pulls gems down, new gems refill from the top, repeat until stable; find_matches scans H+V runs; has_legal_move probes every adjacent swap and auto-reshuffles a dead board. match3.gd renders the gem grid in code, click a gem then an adjacent gem to swap, with a score/moves HUD + chain banner. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (the exact board + score persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in as a standalone game or a minigame inside a larger title.

primitives.note: swap the flat-color gem buttons for real gem/candy sprites (recipes: gem icons via qwen-icon, board backdrop via zit-txt2img)
hook swap/clear/cascade for juice — pop/scale on clear, screen shake on big chains, particle bursts

assetPlanHints: 6 gem / candy icons (one per type id) via qwen-icon
Board frame + background via zit-txt2img
Score / combo / chain HUD chrome via qwen-txt2img

templates.name: Gacha Summon (collection / pity system, 2D)
templates.description: The summon + collection core of a companion-collection game (Genshin/FGO lineage) — on-mission for waifu/companion rosters. The whole GACHA is a GameManager autoload as pure, seedable, headless-testable logic: a wallet of premium gems, published rarity RATES (3★/4★/5★) with a real PITY system — a 5★ rate that ramps from soft pity (74) and is GUARANTEED at hard pity (90), plus a 4★-or-better guaranteed every 10 pulls — a rarity→pool draw, single and 10-pulls that spend gems (stopping when the wallet is dry), and a collection that tracks dupes. pull(count) returns per-pull results {rarity,item,dupe}; current_five_chance() exposes the live pity-adjusted odds. gacha.gd renders the summon screen in code — wallet + pity + next-5★ chance, Pull ×1 / Pull ×10, the last results (rarity-coloured), and the collection by rarity. Swap the POOL const to your own roster. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (wallet + pity + whole collection persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in standalone or as the summon screen of a larger game.

primitives.note: make the 5★/4★ pool real companions — each pulled unit gets a full companion-npcs persona/voice for the roster + chat
swap the text rows for real character cards + rarity frames (recipes: character art via card-creature-art, rarity frames via card-frame, star/gem icons via qwen-icon, banner splash via zit-txt2img)

Rarity frames (3★/4★/5★ borders) via card-frame
